creator: Lu@FreeBSD.org
text:
The bug comes when I use Dummynet (to delay the packets) in the system and try to send a packet with IP option in it. In the end system, I set up a pipe with, say 50 ms delay and then I send IP packets with IP options and through the Dummynet pipe. The pipe is configured as "out", so the hook in "ip_output()" will capture it. The result is that the packet with IP option has incorrect header length field. The packet with IP option should has header length larger than 20 bytes (number 5 in packet header). However, in the above situation, the header length is always set as 20 bytes, so the packet is corrupted.

The problem won't appear if the packet does not go through the Dummynet pipe, or without any IP option field.

I believe other releases of FreeBSD will have a big chance to also have this problem.

Fix: I believe that the problem is a combined effect of the implementation of Dummynet and the IP stack, specifically, the "ip_dummynet.c" and the "ip_output.c". When a packet with IP option first comes to "ip_output()", the option is inserted and the packet header length is adjusted. Then it is captured by Dummynet, and the Dummynet will send it use "ip_output()" again without any option specified this time (the packet has already IP option in it). However, in this second time, "ip_output()" will set 5 (20 bytes) as the default length of the header since it finds that there is no option parameter as input, and thus the packet is corrupted.

One possible fix is to modify the "ip_output()" function a bit. Since Dummynet will leave a specific dummynet tag in the packet, instead of blindly set header length as 5, "ip_output()" can look at the dummynet tag. If it finds the tag, then the packet is from a dummynet pipe and the length should already be valid, and in this case, "ip_output()" does not need to set the length value with default one.

The ip_output.c attached has the fix described above.

Patch attached with submission follows:
How-To-Repeat: set up a Dummynet pipe with some delay, and use "ping -R" to send some IP (ICMP) packet through the pipe, the packet will be corrupted
